column1: George orwell wrote books than any other 20th century writer.
As a student at Eton, he soon showed how clever he was.
As a young man, Orwell left England and became a policeman.
Orwell wanted to do more than journalism in his early writing career.
People living in poor conditions often praised Orwell for his work.
His time in Spain changed his feelings about working-class people.
Orwell wrote Nineteen Eighty-Four years after writing Animal Farm.
Orwell wanted his books to be used in classrooms.
